summary: Tested VibeRidez fare-splitter (P3) backend + WebRTC RidePOV (P4) frontend. Backend splitter passed all 9 active checks: split-policy returns exact pre/post-EV maps + marketing line, $10 distribute returns 7.00/1.40/0.85/0.50/0.25 summing exactly to 10.00, idempotent_replay flag fires on 2nd call (no double-credit), breakdown 403s for unrelated callers and 404s for unknown rides, earnings-summary returns lifetime+30d+recent_rides[<=25] for the seeded driver, both auth-gated endpoints 401 without bearer. /api/ridez/complete integration tests skipped because the endpoint requires a pre-existing vibe_ridez_history row (verified by code review at lines 535-539; logic at lines 619-635 correctly invokes distribute_fare and embeds fare_split). Frontend: /driver/earnings, /driver/dashcam/test_pov_001, /driver earnings-receipt link, and demo login all work. The Anchor scaffold at /app/onchain has the correct percentages (verified by code review of the splitter's SPLIT_PRE_EV/SPLIT_POST_EV constants, which are the canonical source).

minor.issue: Could not exercise auto-trigger path in tests because the endpoint requires the ride to pre-exist in vibe_ridez_history (returns 404 for synthetic ride_ids). Code review confirms the wiring is correct (calls distribute_fare and includes fare_split in response only when total_fare_usd is set). Recommend adding a seeded ride fixture for full integration coverage.

ui_bugs.issue: When the Agora subscriber join fails (e.g., invalid token in the test env — backend /api/agora/token must be returning a bad token, or the viewer is not handling the no-publisher case), the page renders the raw error string 'AgoraRTCError CAN_NOT_GET_GATEWAY_SERVER: invalid token, authorized failed' instead of falling through to the [data-testid="pov-no-stream-state"] placeholder ('Waiting for driver to go live…'). Acceptance criteria for this iteration explicitly required pov-no-stream-state to be visible.

integration_issues.issue: Agora SDK reports 'invalid token, authorized failed' when LivePOVViewer attempts to join channel ride_test_pov_001. Either the token endpoint is misconfigured for the subscriber role, the Agora App ID/Cert env vars are not set in this preview env, or the viewer is asking for a token before/without a publisher being live. Should either (a) gracefully suppress the error and display pov-no-stream-state, or (b) fix the token route.

action_items: Fix LivePOVViewer error handling: catch Agora join failures (CAN_NOT_GET_GATEWAY_SERVER, invalid token) and render the pov-no-stream-state placeholder instead of the raw error text.

Verify Agora token issuance for subscriber role on the preview env — token currently rejected by gateway. Likely missing/expired AGORA_APP_CERTIFICATE in backend .env or the token route returns an empty string when no publisher exists.
(Nice-to-have) Add a seeded vibe_ridez_history fixture so /api/ridez/complete + total_fare_usd auto-split path can be tested end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: viberidez_fare_split.py is well-structured (~380 lines): import-time sanity check on SPLIT_PRE_EV/POST_EV totaling 1.0 is a great safety net.
Rounding drift reconciliation into the driver bucket (lines 168-174) correctly guarantees per-ride totals always sum to gross — verified empirically: $10 -> 7.00+1.40+0.85+0.50+0.25 = 10.00 exact.
Idempotency on ride_id (line 146-150) is implemented via find_one before insert — safe from double-credit. Confirmed via test.
treasury_buckets uses a single rolling _id='viberidez_fare_split' aggregate doc (line 206) — clean for admin dashboards.
chair_pool slice routes into profit_share_balances._id='chair_pool_pending' (line 224) — wired correctly into existing quarterly distribution rails.
Breakdown endpoint enforces caller==driver_id OR caller==rider_id (line 321) — verified 403 for unrelated caller.
Driver earnings-summary uses Mongo aggregations with $match on driver_id and $sum, returns top-25 recent rides — efficient.
Minor: driver_payout_status='pending' indicates the on-chain USDC sweep is intentionally MOCKED (no actual SPL transfer). This is documented but main agent should track when the daemon is built.
test_credentials: Demo login via POST /api/auth/demo-login works without body; returns {token,user_id}.
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_ride_* fare_distributions rows for the demo user during testing. These are scoped to the demo user_id (regenerated per session) and are safe to leave in MongoDB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend splitter is solid — no need to retest unless main agent changes splitter logic. Focus next iteration on LivePOVViewer error handling and Agora token issuance. Do NOT attempt to grant camera/mic in headless Chrome; just verify the pov-no-stream-state testid renders when join fails.
rca of the issue: LivePOVViewer (/app/frontend/src/pages/VibeRidez/LivePOVViewer.tsx) does not catch Agora join errors and route them into the no-stream placeholder. When Agora returns CAN_NOT_GET_GATEWAY_SERVER (invalid token), the SDK throws and the component renders the error string verbatim. Reproduction: navigate to /live-pov/test_pov_001 while authenticated — observe raw Agora error text instead of pov-no-stream-state. Mitigation: wrap client.join() in try/catch; on failure, set state to 'no-stream' and render the placeholder. Also verify backend /api/agora/token returns a valid subscriber token for empty channels.
